questions.q: How many years did the Pandavas rule after the war before renouncing the throne?
questions.explanation: After thirty-six years of rule, the news of Krishna's death and the Yadavas' end moved the Pandavas to renounce the world.

questions.q: Toward which great mountain did the Pandavas walk on their last journey?
questions.explanation: The Pandavas crossed the Himalayas heading for Mount Meru, the axis of the world, on the Mahaprasthana.

questions.q: Who was the first of the travelers to fall on the northward march?
questions.explanation: Draupadi fell first, and the brothers then dropped one by one in reverse order of age.

questions.q: What creature joined the Pandavas and followed them on the final journey?
questions.explanation: A dog attached itself to the party as they left Hastinapura and stayed with Yudhishthira to the very end.

questions.q: Who alone of the seven travelers remained standing at the journey's end?
questions.explanation: Only Yudhishthira, with the dog beside him, was still walking when Indra's chariot descended.

questions.q: Whom did Yudhishthira crown at Hastinapura before the Pandavas departed?
questions.explanation: Abhimanyu's son Parikshit, the last thread of the Kuru line, was installed as king before the departure.

questions.q: Who was the faithful dog at heaven's gate finally revealed to be?
options: Agni, come to reclaim the Gandiva
Narada, watching the Kuru line end
questions.explanation: The dog was the god Dharma in disguise, testing whether his son would abandon a devoted creature for heaven.

questions.q: Who arrived with a celestial chariot to carry Yudhishthira to heaven?
questions.explanation: Indra, king of the gods, came in his chariot to take Yudhishthira to heaven in his own body.

questions.q: What condition did Yudhishthira set before he would board the chariot to heaven?
options: His brothers must be revived on the spot
Draupadi must first be crowned in heaven
His mortal sins must first be erased
The faithful dog must be allowed to come too
questions.explanation: Yudhishthira refused heaven itself unless the dog that had walked with him could enter as well.

questions.q: Which book of the Mahabharata describes the Pandavas' great departure?
questions.explanation: The Mahaprasthanika Parva, the seventeenth book, narrates the great setting-forth toward Meru.

questions.q: Who fell second on the journey, immediately after Draupadi?
questions.explanation: Sahadeva, the youngest brother, fell after Draupadi, and the others followed in reverse order of age.

questions.q: What flaw did Yudhishthira name as the cause of Draupadi's fall?
options: Her pride in her fire-born beauty
Her unforgiving hatred of the Kauravas
Her partiality for Arjuna above her other husbands
Her vanity as empress of Indraprastha
questions.explanation: Though wed to five, Draupadi had favored Arjuna in her heart, and that partiality brought her down first.

questions.q: Why did Sahadeva fall on the mountain path?
options: He had concealed the future he could foresee
He was proud of his wisdom, thinking none his equal
He had loved his mother Madri above his dharma
He had sworn a vow that he failed to keep
questions.explanation: Yudhishthira said Sahadeva fell because he believed no one equalled him in wisdom.

questions.q: What failing brought Nakula down on the journey?
options: His secret envy of his brothers
His attachment to his horses and sword
His failure to defend Draupadi in the dice hall
His vanity in believing none matched his beauty
questions.explanation: Nakula, famed as the handsomest man of his age, fell for his pride in his own beauty.

questions.q: What failing did Yudhishthira name when Arjuna fell?
options: His slaying of Karna against the rules of war
His pride in the Gandiva above the gods
His boast that he could destroy all foes in a single day, never fulfilled
His envy of Ekalavya's surpassing skill
questions.explanation: Arjuna fell for the pride of his unfulfilled boast that he would consume all his enemies in one day.

questions.q: What flaws did Yudhishthira name when mighty Bhima fell?
options: Gluttony and boastfulness about his strength
Wrath that never once cooled in his life
questions.explanation: Bhima fell because he ate without thought for others and vaunted his strength too much.

questions.q: Who confronted the Pandavas at the journey's start and demanded that Arjuna give up the Gandiva?
options: Vyasa, closing the age of weapons
Agni, who had first procured the bow
questions.explanation: Agni stood before them like a mountain of flame and bade Arjuna return the Gandiva, which he had obtained from Varuna.

questions.q: What was done with the Gandiva and its two inexhaustible quivers?
options: They were buried beneath Mount Meru
They were cast into the sea, returned to Varuna
They were burned up in Agni's own flame
They were hung once more in the shami tree
questions.explanation: At Agni's command Arjuna threw the great bow and quivers into the waters, restoring them to Varuna.

questions.q: What did the Pandavas see when their long circuit brought them to the western coast?
options: The submerged iron pestle of the curse
Krishna waiting for them on the shore
A bridge of stone across the sea
questions.explanation: On the western shore they beheld Krishna's golden city of Dwarka covered over by the sea.

questions.q: What objection did Indra raise against the dog entering heaven?
options: The dog was a demon in disguise
The dog must first be tested by fire
Heaven has no place for dogs, whose presence defiles offerings
Only those who shed the body may enter heaven
questions.explanation: Indra protested that dog-keepers win no heaven and that a dog's gaze defiles sacred offerings, but Yudhishthira would not yield.

questions.q: As his companions fell one by one, what did Yudhishthira do?
options: He performed funeral rites for each before walking on
He explained each one's flaw to Bhima and walked on without looking back
He carried their ashes onward in his water pot
He kept vigil over each of them until nightfall
questions.explanation: To Bhima's anguished questions Yudhishthira named each one's failing and continued north, never turning his head.

questions.q: Who was installed as ruler at Indraprastha before the Pandavas departed?
questions.explanation: Vajra, Krishna's great-grandson and survivor of the Yadava massacre, was set on the throne of Indraprastha.

questions.q: Whom did Yudhishthira see enthroned in glory when he first entered heaven?
questions.explanation: To his outrage, Yudhishthira found Duryodhana seated in splendor while he could not yet see his brothers.

questions.q: What was the final test Yudhishthira faced after reaching heaven?
options: To renounce his memory of Draupadi
To judge Duryodhana's right to heaven
An illusion of hell where his brothers' voices begged him to stay
To pardon Ashwatthama for the night massacre
questions.explanation: Led to a reeking darkness where his loved ones seemed to suffer, he chose to remain with them, and the illusion vanished.

questions.q: How did Yudhishthira finally shed his mortal body?
options: It dissolved when he forgave Duryodhana
Yama drew the breath from him painlessly
It burned away in the fire of Indra's chariot
He bathed in the celestial Ganga at the gods' bidding
questions.explanation: At the gods' direction he plunged into the heavenly Ganga and rose in a divine form, free of grief and enmity.

questions.q: Who was appointed preceptor of the young king Parikshit when the Pandavas left?
questions.explanation: Parikshit was placed under the tutelage of the immortal Kripacharya, while Yuyutsu was asked to oversee the kingdom's affairs.

questions.q: In which direction did the Pandavas first set out before finally turning north?
options: West, toward the ruins of Dwarka
East, toward the sea where the Gandiva was surrendered
They walked north from the very first step
questions.explanation: They went east first to the sea, where Agni claimed the Gandiva, then circled south and west past drowned Dwarka before turning north.

questions.q: Where had Dharma said he previously tested Yudhishthira, before the test as the dog?
options: In the dice hall of Hastinapura
At the lake in the Dvaita forest, as the Yaksha
On the night of Ashwatthama's raid
questions.explanation: Revealing himself, Dharma recalled testing his son at the Dvaita forest lake, where Yudhishthira chose Nakula's life.

questions.q: Which sage addressed Yudhishthira in heaven when he raged at Duryodhana's honored seat?
questions.explanation: Narada told him that heavenly enmities must cease and that Duryodhana had earned his place by a hero's death.

questions.q: Why, according to the Svargarohana Parva, did Duryodhana merit a place in heaven?
options: Gandhari's austerities had ransomed his sins
Krishna's dying mercy had redeemed him
He had fulfilled kshatriya dharma, dying fearlessly in battle
His final repentance at the lake had cleansed him
questions.explanation: Duryodhana won the worlds of heroes by meeting death unflinchingly in battle, the kshatriya's sanctifying end.
